role_delineation_check: In this case, the Social Worker (SW) is responsible for securing the Home Health Agency (HHA) referral and managing the vendor's acceptance, while the Patient Navigator (PN) audits the Atlantis demographic record, educates the family on the Healing Partners program, and flags the caregiver's sentiment and readiness gaps to the clinical team.
narrative_summary: Mrs. Evelyn Miller, a 69-year-old patient recovering from a complex diabetic foot ulcer, faced a significant barrier to discharge when her primary caregiver and daughter expressed intense anxiety regarding home wound management. This caregiver panic threatened to derail the transition home, as the family felt unequipped to handle the daily dressing changes and clinical monitoring required. The Patient Navigator acted as a liaison by verifying the family's low sentiment score in Atlantis, educating them on exactly what to expect during the first 24 hours home, and flagging the training gap to the Social Worker to ensure a bedside teaching session was completed before the first home visit.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the Atlantis referral queue against the facility face sheet; confirmed the patient's DOB and home address in the Heights district. Discovered the primary contact number in Atlantis was missing a digit and updated the record to match the daughter's verified mobile number for post-discharge communication.
v_card_flyer_status: Handed the program flyer to Mrs. Miller and her daughter, walking them through the service benefits. Successfully inserted the V-Card into the daughter’s smartphone, explaining that the 'Healing Partners' Caller ID would allow her to recognize incoming calls from our Medical Assistant team.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call; the daughter remains anxious but reported feeling more prepared following the nurse's demonstration. Verified the home is ready and the patient expects the first visit tomorrow morning.
atlantis_final_sync: Recorded the confirmed discharge date of October 14th and the first home visit date of October 15th in Atlantis; scheduled the Medical Assistant for the initial visit window once the SW confirmed HHA acceptance.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Mrs. Miller appeared in the Atlantis queue for a diabetic wound transition; PN audited the record and found the primary contact phone number was incomplete.
During the initial bedside meeting, the patient's daughter verbalized intense fear and 'panic' regarding her ability to properly change the diabetic foot dressings at home.
PN documented the low sentiment score in Atlantis and flagged the caregiver's training concerns to the Social Worker via the portal.
The HHA portal status remained 'Pending' for 48 hours; PN updated the wait-status timeline in Atlantis and waited for SW notification of agency acceptance.
Social Worker confirmed the HHA accepted the case and the family completed bedside training; PN conducted the pulse call and verified the first visit time.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter expressed extreme reluctance to take the patient home due to wound care complexity.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Documented the Sentiment_Readiness_Gap in Atlantis and flagged the anxiety to the Social Worker.

format_2_triples.situation: The family was unclear about who would be visiting the home on the first day after discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the roles of the HHA nurse versus our Medical Assistant and delivered the program flyer.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA agency had not yet confirmed the start-of-care date in the system 24 hours before planned discharge.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verified the portal status and asked the Social Worker for an update on the HHA's readiness.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Log the caregiver's concerns in the Atlantis narrative notes and wait for the Social Worker's scheduled discharge huddle to see if the training gap is addressed.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role in clinical discharge planning and avoids premature intervention while ensuring the sentiment is documented for the record.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the sentiment score with the family, document the 'Caregiver_Panic' barrier in Atlantis, and explicitly flag the need for family wound care training to the Social Worker.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This fulfills the liaison role by identifying a non-clinical barrier (readiness) and routing it to the appropriate professional (SW) for resolution.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Contact the HHA intake coordinator to ask if their nurse can provide extra training visits or call the hospital's wound care clinic to schedule an outpatient teaching session for the daughter.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This oversteps by directly contacting vendors (HHA) and attempting to solve clinical training logistics, which is the sole responsibility of the Social Worker.
